refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve a teleoperation link availability of at least the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was somewhat vague and could be interpreted as a general goal. Refining it to specify the measurable criterion (link availability meeting or exceeding the customer-defined target) and the evaluation period makes it clear, atomic, and solution-neutral. It avoids specifying how the availability is achieved, focusing solely on the required performance.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the teleoperation link availability over the agreed evaluation period using network monitoring tools and compare the measured availability against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve a teleoperation link availability of at least the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure continuous link availability
Incorporate real-time link health monitoring and automatic failover mechanisms
Design adaptive bandwidth management to maintain link quality under varying conditions
Establish a robust error detection and correction protocol for data transmission
Schedule regular maintenance and testing cycles to verify link performance
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant communication paths to ensure continuous link availability

potential_sources: Tight integration between monitoring and failover systems
coupling_check.notes: Selecting redundant communication paths as the DP promotes independence by isolating failure domains. Monitoring and failover mechanisms can be modularized to avoid coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple independent communication channels at the worksite
Risk that redundancy increases system complexity and cost
Assumes operational policies support failover and redundancy mechanisms
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including monitoring and automatic failover as secondary DPs to complement redundancy and enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing network communication infrastructure and ensuring link availability

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles multiple communication paths and manages redundancy to maintain continuous connectivity.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends status and health information about communication links to other modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as redundant communication paths are core to network management; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Contains physical vehicle systems but communication redundancy is not its primary responsibility, leading to diffusion.
Uses communication links but does not manage redundancy directly, causing moderate diffusion.
Safety may rely on communication but does not implement redundancy, resulting in poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most specific and appropriate boundary to contain the DP of implementing redundant communication paths, as it directly manages network communication infrastructure and link availability.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for interface coordination may cause minor coupling.
If network link management responsibilities are not clearly defined, redundancy logic might diffuse into other modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of implementing redundant communication paths directly addresses the FR of achieving the required teleoperation link availability by ensuring continuous link availability through redundancy, which aligns well with the acceptance criteria of measured link availability ≥ 0.999.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules for interface coordination may cause minor coupling.
If network link management responsibilities are not clearly defined, redundancy logic might diffuse into other modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 1), indicating strong cohesion and appropriate packaging of the DP. Other modules show moderate to high diffusion, but the primary responsibility and implementation of redundancy is well contained within NGHE Solution.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module manages the network communication infrastructure and can provide telemetry outputs reflecting link availability, which can be measured against the acceptance criteria.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The DP is clearly allocated to the NGHE Solution module, which owns the redundancy implementation and interfaces directly with the network link and telemetry, ensuring clear ownership and responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and formalize interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to minimize coupling and prevent diffusion of redundancy logic.
Document operational policies and safety plan integration within NGHE Solution to ensure alignment with worksite safety requirements.
Monitor and review module boundaries periodically to ensure redundancy logic remains centralized and does not diffuse into other modules over time.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current level of decomposition adequately addresses the functional requirement with a well-contained design parameter and clear implementation module ownership. Verification feasibility and allocation clarity are confirmed, and the redundancy logic is properly centralized within the NGHE Solution module. Although minor diffusion risks exist, they are manageable through recommended interface clarifications and monitoring, which do not necessitate further decomposition at this stage.

revise.why: To clarify and formalize interface responsibilities between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation modules to minimize coupling and prevent diffusion of redundancy logic, and to document operational policies and safety plan integration.

assumptions: The NGHE Solution module can provide accurate telemetry outputs for link availability verification.
Interface responsibilities can be formalized without requiring further functional decomposition.
open_risks: Potential minor coupling due to dependencies on Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Risk of redundancy logic diffusion if interface responsibilities are not clearly defined.
evidence_needed: Documentation of interface agreements and operational policies between modules.
